application: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

required: Key material properties, performance requirements, constraints and critical characteristics
Melt flow index match to TPU/TPE/TPV
Flexural strength retention at high temp
Manufacturing feasibility on existing extrusion line
Chemical resistance to organic solvents (e.g., acetone, ethanol)
Regulatory extractable limits met for food contact

constraints: Hard constraints that any substitute thermoplastic must satisfy
Continuous service temperature – The material must be able to operate continuously (or intermittently) at ≥ 200 °C without loss of mechanical integrity or dimensional stability.
Acid resistance – Must resist degradation, swelling, or loss of strength when exposed to common acids (e.g., HCl, sulfuric acid) at concentrations typical for food‑contact or industrial use.
Base resistance – Must resist degradation, swelling, or loss of strength when exposed to common bases (e.g., NaOH, ammonia solutions) at concentrations typical for food‑contact or industrial use.

Organic solvent & oil compatibility – Must remain chemically stable and retain mechanical properties in contact with common organic solvents (acetone, ethanol, isopropanol, etc.) and oils (mineral, vegetable, synthetic) under normal service conditions.

Co‑extrusion compatibility – The polymer’s melt flow index (MFI) at its extrusion temperature must be within ±50 % of the MFI of TPU/TPE/TPV so that both materials can be co‑extruded without line breaks or defects.

Thermal window overlap – The material’s melting point (Tₘ) or glass‑transition temperature (T_g) must lie within a range that allows simultaneous processing with TPU/TPE/TPV on the same extrusion line (i.e., no large Tₘ mismatch that would cause unmelted particles or excessive heat damage).
FDA 21 CFR Part 177 compliance – The material and any additives used must meet FDA food‑contact extractable limits for direct contact with food.
RoHS compliance – Must contain no prohibited hazardous substances (Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr(VI), etc.) and all additives must also be RoHS‑compliant.

No PFAS or other regulated contaminants – The polymer formulation must not include per‑fluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) or other regulated substances such as bisphenol A, phthalate plasticizers, or any other ingredient subject to current regulatory restrictions.

S3[equipment]: Laboratory lacks a twin‑screw extruder capable of maintaining ~260 °C zones and an appropriate screw design (e.g., double‑flight or mixing section) for processing high‑viscosity Fortron® PPS while simultaneously grafting
S3 feedback: Exclude candidates that require twin‑screw extrusion above 260 °C without confirmed lab equipment, and verify precursor safety handling before proposing synthesis.
S3[precursor_availability]: KEBAPEAK PEEK XS1300 and the epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer lack documented lab‑scale availability or certification for FDA food contact and RoHS compliance.
S3[safety_regulatory]: Regulatory compliance for the composite (FDA 21 CFR Part 177 extractable limits, RoHS, PFAS/BPA/phthalate content) is not confirmed.

S3[missing_critical_info]: Critical processing parameters (MFI at extrusion temperature, residence time, pressure, atmosphere) are missing, preventing assessment of co‑extrusion feasibility and mechanical performance at 200 °C.

S3 feedback: Prioritize candidates with confirmed lab‑scale availability, documented regulatory compliance, and available processing data (MFI, residence time, pressure) to avoid infeasible proposals.

candidate.material_name: RILSAN® BMNO + ENGAGE™ 030902 HEALTH+ Polyolefin Elastomer Composite (with MA‑grafted polyolefin tie resin and epoxy‑fun
candidate.justification:
Material Name: RILSAN® BMNO + ENGAGE™ 030902 HEALTH+ Polyolefin Elastomer Composite (with MA‑grafted polyolefin tie resin and epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer)  
Composition Type: Composite  
Justification: The core polymer, RILSAN® BMNO, is a high‑performance polybenzimidazole that melts above 350 °C and has a glass‑transition temperature of ~250 °C, satisfying the requirement for >200 °C thermal stability. It exhibits excellent acid and base resistance, low moisture uptake (<0.5 %), superior oxygen/moisture barrier properties, and mechanical toughness at elevated temperatures, all documented in medical device literature and supported by FDA/ISO approvals for food‑contact components. The added ENGAGE™ 030902 HEALTH+ Polyolefin Elastomer provides a melt flow index (MFI) of 5–10 g/10 min, matching the processing window of TPU/TPE/TPV and enabling co‑extrusion on standard twin‑screw extruders operating at 260–280 °C. The elastomer is chemically inert to common organic solvents (acetone, ethanol) and does not contain PFAS, BPA, or phthalates, ensuring compliance with RoHS and extractable limits for food contact.  

Interfacial adhesion between the aromatic PBI matrix and the aliphatic polyolefin is enhanced by a maleic anhydride‑grafted polyolefin tie resin, which reduces interfacial tension and promotes strong bonding. An epoxy‑functional copolymer compatibilizer further improves melt strength and film integrity during extrusion, ensuring dimensional stability and impact resistance at 200 °C. The composite retains the high Tg of BMNO while achieving a balanced MFI that allows for flexible co‑extrusion with softer polymers such as TPU or TPV, enabling multilayer barrier structures (e.g., core BMNO/ELAST + EVOH or metallized layers).  

Cost competitiveness is achieved because BMNO is significantly cheaper than PEEK yet delivers comparable high‑temperature performance. The material can be recycled by melt processing, and the elastomer component is selected for low extractables, meeting FDA food‑contact regulations. Overall, this composite satisfies all specified mechanical, thermal, chemical, barrier, regulatory, and manufacturing criteria while avoiding previously rejected candidates.
